title: Raspberry frangipane tart
subtitle: Sweet pastry with almond cream and fresh raspberries
excerpt: A 23 cm tart with a sweet shortcrust shell, a layer of raspberry jam, almond cream baked golden, and fresh raspberries pressed in before baking. Eight slices. The frangipane rises around the fruit; the jam underneath gives a concentrated fruit base.
sourceNotes: Frangipane (creme d'amandes) tarts are documented in French and British patisserie from at least the seventeenth century. Recipes appear in Marie-Antoine Careme, Le Patissier Royal Parisien (1815, public domain), and in Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861, Project Gutenberg #10136), which gives the almond cream filling with fruit variations. Cross-referenced with pre-1928 British and French patisserie manuals available through national library digital archives.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Bake the day before. Keeps refrigerated for 2 days.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven. Conventional 185°C. The frangipane should be golden and set; a skewer should come out clean.
baking.decoratingTechnique: dust with icing sugar once cooled

glossaryTerms.definition: An almond cream made from equal weights of butter, sugar, ground almonds, and egg. Baked in a pastry shell, it rises into a moist, spongy layer with a slight crust on top. The flavour is rich and almond-forward; the texture falls between a cake and a custard. Frangipane absorbs moisture from any fruit pressed into it during baking, which concentrates the fruit flavour and prevents the filling from drying out. It is distinct from almond paste (marzipan): frangipane is a baked cream; marzipan is a raw confection.

content.text: A raspberry frangipane tart layers sweet pastry, a scraping of jam, and
with fresh raspberries pressed in before baking. The pastry is partially blind-baked first. Eight slices. The pastry needs an hour in the fridge before lining the tin.
Rub the cold butter into the flour and icing sugar until crumbly. Mix in the egg yolk and cold water until the dough just comes together. Flatten, wrap in cling film, and chill for 1 hour.

Heat the oven to 190°C fan (200°C conventional). Roll the chilled pastry to 3 mm and line the tart tin. Trim, chill for 15 minutes, then line with paper and beans. Bake for 15 minutes, then remove the paper and beans and bake for a further 5 minutes. The pastry should be set but barely coloured. Reduce the oven to 175°C fan (185°C conventional). Spread the jam over the base of the warm shell.

Beat the softened butter and caster sugar until pale. Beat in the eggs one at a time, then fold in the ground almonds and vanilla. Spoon the frangipane over the jam and smooth to an even layer. Press the raspberries into the surface, spaced evenly. Scatter the flaked almonds over the top.

Bake at 175°C fan for 35 to 40 minutes, until the frangipane is golden and a skewer inserted into the centre comes out clean. Cool in the tin for 20 minutes, then remove and dust with icing sugar. Serve warm or at room temperature.
